reasoning: sanitize_url() is confirmed at line 749 in array_outline_sanitize(). The function is deprecated since WordPress 6.1 in favor of esc_url_raw(). The rest of metaboxes.php consistently uses esc_url_raw() (lines 625, 626, 633, 650) making this a clear inconsistency. Reachability is correctly characterized as admin-only: save_meta_boxes() enforces a nonce check (wp_verify_nonce at line 575-577) and a capability check (current_user_can('edit_post') at line 613) before this code path is exercised. There is no exploitable vulnerability — the deprecated function does sanitize URLs and is functionally equivalent to esc_url_raw() in current WordPress versions. The finding is a valid code-quality/maintenance issue: one use of a deprecated API where the rest of the codebase uses the modern replacement. Severity low is correct.
